title: Your first rep

spec.title: Your first rep
spec.notes_for_generator: Write a 5-bullet summary of your week. Dead simple, instant value. The learner has never used AI for work before. Make this so easy it feels almost silly — that's the point. The output should be something they can actually use today.

content:
SUBJECT: AI Gym Day 1: Let's get your first rep done

Most people's first AI prompt is either too vague to be useful or too long to bother finishing. Today's is neither.

Quick scope note before we start: when I say AI in this programme, I mostly mean chat-based large language models — LLMs — that can read, write, summarise, reason, and help structure text. This is not a tour of every kind of AI. We are not doing image generation, predictive analytics, voice cloning, or automation platforms. You can use any text-based LLM you have access to. The habit matters more than the logo. The first 14 days are free because the job at the start is simple: get the reps going. If the habit starts to stick, The 90-Day AI Habit gives you the longer runway a real working habit usually needs.

TODAY'S WORKOUT
───────────────
✍️ CREATION | 🟢 WARM-UP | ⏱ 5–10 min

THE BRIEF:
It's the end of the week. You need a short summary of what you've actually done — useful for a status update, a check-in with your manager, or just your own records. You've got it all in your head. The AI's job is to turn your rough notes into something clean and readable. Five bullets. Done.

YOUR REPS:
1. Open any chat-based LLM you have access to — ChatGPT, Claude, Gemini, Copilot, Perplexity, or your approved work tool. The brand doesn't matter as long as it can read and write text. Use only material you're allowed to put into that tool, and anonymise anything sensitive. Then send it this prompt, filling in the brackets with your own details:

*"Summarise my week as 5 bullet points. Each bullet should be one sentence, written in a professional but plain tone. Here's what I did this week: [paste or type a rough list of your week — even messy notes or half-sentences are fine]."*

2. Read the five bullets back. Ask yourself: does each one sound like something you actually did, or has the model made it vague or generic? If any bullet feels hollow, send a follow-up: *"Bullet [X] is too vague. The specific thing I did was [add detail]. Rewrite just that bullet."*

3. Now ask the model to rewrite the same five bullets in a shorter format — one line each, suitable for a quick message to your team or manager. Notice how the tone shifts when you change the stated audience.

SUCCESS CRITERIA:
Your output is good if:
• Each bullet describes a specific action or outcome — not "worked on various tasks" but "completed first draft of Q3 supplier review"
• Someone who wasn't with you this week could read the list and understand what you actually delivered
• The final version feels like something you'd genuinely send, not something you'd have to apologise for

💡 COACHING NOTE:
The most important habit in this programme is giving the model your raw material, not asking it to invent things. Notice that Rep 1 works because *you* supply the content — the model just shapes it. This is true for almost every useful LLM task: your context in, usable output out. An LLM with nothing to work from will make things up or go generic. An LLM with your actual notes will give you something worth reading. That principle scales to reports, presentations, emails, and beyond.

──────────
Use only material you are allowed to share with your chosen LLM. Anonymise anything sensitive.
Share the programme: https://harrysharman.com/projects/ai-habit/
Found this useful? You can leave a tip: https://ko-fi.com/theaihabit
review_notes: Clarified that AI Habit focuses on chat-based large language models (LLMs), while using AI as public shorthand.
Kept exercises model-agnostic and usable with any text-based LLM or approved work tool.
Reworked research/reasoning language to avoid over-trusting model confidence or hidden chain-of-thought.
Reframed AI Habit as a grounded free 14-day starter block with a paid continuation for deeper 60-90 day habit-building, rather than a validation experiment.

Positioned the paid continuation as The 90-Day AI Habit: a structured applied-LLM programme covering writing/editing, strategy/decision-making, research/assessment, management/delegation, personal productivity, and practical outputs.
Simplified the 90-day commercial route: join individually, bring a small group for a discounted group subscription, or use the workplace launch pack to get an employer to pay.
Added ethical upgrade nudges to Days 3, 7, and 10: endowed progress, career identity, and momentum protection for The 90-Day AI Habit.
Mechanical repair pass: normalised workout badge line after scheduler verification.
